chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the features and key specifications for Rheonics Type-SR inline viscometers and density sensors, detailing the SRV viscometer and the SRD density and viscosity meter.
chunks.extracted_text:
1. Rheonics Type-SR Features overview

SRV: Symmetric torsional resonator
Rheonics inline viscometer
Viscosity range:
0.5 – 50 000 cP
Viscosity accuracy:
5% of reading (standard)
1% or higher available
Reproducibility:
better than 0.1% of reading

Density range:
0.0 – 4.0 g/cc
0.0 – 33.4 lb/gal
Density accuracy:
0.001 g/cc
0.008 lb/gal
SRD: Symmetric torsional resonator
Rheonics inline density and viscosity meter
Reproducibility:
better than 0.1% of reading

rheonics
© Rheonics | Global Partners Training
3
key_facts: Rheonics offers Type-SR devices, which include the SRV inline viscometer and the SRD inline density and viscosity meter.
Both SRV and SRD models utilize symmetric torsional resonator technology.
The SRV inline viscometer has a viscosity range of 0.5 to 50,000 cP.
The standard viscosity accuracy for the SRV is 5% of reading, with 1% or higher accuracy options available.
The SRD inline density and viscosity meter has a density range of 0.0 to 4.0 g/cc (or 0.0 to 33.4 lb/gal).
The SRD meter achieves a density accuracy of 0.001 g/cc (or 0.008 lb/gal).
Both the SRV and SRD models demonstrate a reproducibility better than 0.1% of reading for their measurements.

5
key_facts: The page describes the architecture of an Ignition system.
Rheonics SRD units (SRD 1 through SRD 6) are connected to PLCs (PLC 1 and PLC 2).
Each SRD unit supports Modbus RTU via RS485 and Modbus TCP via Ethernet, and includes reset and SD-Card functionalities.
All mentioned communication and features apply to all SRD versions.
PLCs feed data to an Ignition server, which is connected to a database.
The Ignition server enables access via mobile devices, web-based applications, and panel views.

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines the benefits of integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition for improved downtime management, focusing on data integration, visualization, multi-instrument data, and process traceability.
chunks.extracted_text:
4. What can you achieve by integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition?

Key benefits:
• Quick integration of sensor data
• Real-time visualization dashboards
• Actionable multi-instrument data
• Complete Traceability across process

DOWNTIME MANAGEMENT

Complete Traceability across process
Quick integration of sensor data
Real-time visualization dashboards
Actionable multi-instrument data

© Rheonics | Global Partners Training
6
key_facts: Integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition offers key benefits including quick integration of sensor data.
Real-time visualization dashboards are a key benefit of integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition.
Actionable multi-instrument data can be achieved by integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition.
Complete traceability across the process is a benefit provided by integrating Rheonics sensors with Ignition.

The integration supports downtime management by enabling quick integration of sensor data, real-time visualization dashboards, actionable multi-instrument data, and complete traceability across the process.

key_facts: Rheonics resources can be found on the Inductive Automation Ignition Exchange website.
Users should navigate to inductiveautomation.com/exchange to find the Ignition Exchange website.
To find Rheonics' resources, type 'rheonics' into the search bar on the Ignition Exchange.
The search result will show the 'Rheonics Viscosity and Density meter' template package, which includes resources for Viscosity meter SRV and Density meter SRD.
The template package is categorized under 'Instrument Interface' and is suitable for 'Beginner' users.

The 'Others Folder' contains a PDF with instructions and additional resources.
The 'Projects Folder' contains three project files to assist users in integrating Rheonics sensors into their processes.
The 'Tags Folder' provides UDT (User-Defined Tag) definitions.
UDT definitions are used to simplify sensor configuration in Ignition.
The described package is named 'rheonics_viscosity_and_density_meter.1.0.2' and is located in 'C > Documents'.
The slide is part of 'Global Partners Training' by Rheonics.

The Ignition Gateway is currently operating under an expiring trial license, with an option to reset for two more hours.

key_facts: The page demonstrates how to create a new device in Ignition Gateway.
A Rheonics device, configured as Modbus TCP for viscosity, is shown with a 'Connecting' status.
A 'Sample_Device' configured as a 'Programmable Device Simulator' is shown with a 'Running' status.

The Ignition trial is expired but can be reset for two more hours.

key_facts: Rheonics Modbus TCP parameters can be configured through an Ignition Gateway interface.
After saving configuration changes for a Rheonics device, it should take a few seconds to connect.
A Rheonics device named 'Rheonics1' is configured as a Modbus TCP viscosity meter and is currently in a 'Connected' status.
The default port for Modbus communication is 502.
The default communication timeout is 2,000 ms.

Modbus addresses for various parameters (DLG, TR, VLG, SST, SSN, KVS) are configured, indicating different Modbus types like Input Register (Float), Input Register (Int16), and Holding Register (Double).

Add a new project using the material available on Rheonics Package downloaded from Ignition exchange website

* Start by clicking on the Import Project button.
* Select the Rheonics project template, then enter a new project name, such as Rheonics Project Template Webinar.
* Click on the Import Project button at the bottom to proceed.
* Once the project is imported, click Open to access all the templates associated with the Rheonics sensor.

Finally enter username and password to launch the Ignition designer.

[Footer]
  rheonics
  © Rheonics | Global Partners Training
  12
key_facts: New projects in Ignition Designer Launcher are added using Rheonics Package materials downloaded from the Ignition exchange website.
The process involves clicking the 'Import Project' button, selecting a Rheonics project template, and entering a project name.
After importing, the project can be opened to access all templates associated with the Rheonics sensor.
Launching the Ignition designer requires entering a username and password.

key_facts: The page describes how to map variables in Ignition Designer, specifically for Rheonics products.
Variable mapping is based on registers assigned during the OPC client setup.
The Ignition Designer software interface is shown, detailing project browser, tag browser, vision property editor, and component palette.
Rheonics uses Ignition OPC UA Server for data communication, with examples like VLG1 and VLG2 tags.
The system can monitor Kinematic Viscosity, Sensor serial number, Sensor Status, Temperature, and Viscosity.
A displayed viscosity value of -2.0000 CP is shown on a Viscometer SRV display component.
The software is running in trial mode with 1 hour, 29 minutes, and 23 seconds remaining.
warnings_notes: Quality: Bad ("Bad: Timeout: The operation timed out.") - This indicates a communication error or timeout with the OPC UA server at the timestamp 1/23/99, 7:00:00 PM PET.
